game_name: Dungeons & Dragons: Warriors of the Eternal Sun

danger_signals.visual_form: Sky tears open, ground shakes violently
danger_signals.meaning: Catastrophic world-state shift; supernatural danger imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: Beastmen, lizardmen, goblins rendered in sprite form during combat
danger_signals.meaning: Combat engagement required; threat to party survival

danger_signals.visual_form: Townspeople increasingly hostile demeanor (narrative-driven, visual via NPC behavior changes)
danger_signals.meaning: Invisible supernatural influence at work; trust breakdown; world under threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Transition from isometric world view to turn-based tactical grid with white selection boxes
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger; game state shifted to strategic combat
Permanent loss; game over state or party member casualty

danger_signals.visual_form: Brilliant red sun in Eternal Sun valley environment
danger_signals.meaning: Alien/unnatural world-state; isolation from home realm

safety_signals.visual_form: Duke Barrik's castle as home base structure
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven; faction anchor; human allied territory

safety_signals.visual_form: King's advisor character remains in castle
safety_signals.meaning: Trustworthy guide; knowledge source; resistance against corruption

safety_signals.visual_form: Real-time party movement in isometric view with no combat indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Safe exploration mode; no immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Loot drops and XP gain messages after successful combat
safety_signals.meaning: Party progress; victory reinforcement; resource accumulation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Humans rendered as corrupted/hostile NPCs late game
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle as central settlement; human architecture; civilized lands

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goblin sprite renders during combat encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Siege warfare context; antagonistic positioning at game start
Cave dwellings; artifact-rich environment; tribal organization

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Lizardmen sprite variants in swamp combat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Swampland habitat; reptilian architecture; knowledge keepers
Jungle temples; ancient ruins; ceremonial sites; artifact deposits
Volcano region; interdimensional gateway; zoo-like world structure
Psychological corruption spreading from unknown source

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Isometric overworld with castle, armies in siege formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Duke's forces vs. goblin army in active warfare; NPC hostility limited to enemy factions

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-rupture: Stranded in Eternal Sun valley
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red sun overhead; impossibly tall valley cliffs; alien landscape; same castle relocated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party shifts to exploration mode; NPCs initially cooperative; environmental hazards replace army threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: NPC behavior changes toward party; castle interior shows signs of unrest
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Townspeople become increasingly insane and hostile; party treated as threat; Marmillian isolation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Castle appears abandoned; Marmillian as sole remaining presence; world theme shifts to horror/revelation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Exposition reveals invisible Burrower influence; party reframed as saviors; final dungeon accessible

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final state: Burrower defeated, realm restored
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Presumed return to normal sky; Eternal Sun influence negated; transportation spell activation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party summons Ka; people restored to safety; dimensional separation healed

environmental_storytelling.example: Beastmen caves contain artifacts from different time periods, signaling this world is a zoo/collection space rather than natural realm

environmental_storytelling.technique: Progressive NPC behavior shift as environmental indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Townspeople's increasing hostility without player action communicates invisible supernatural force; madness spreads visually through behavior
Caves → Swamps → Jungle → Volcano progression reveals deeper mysteries; each region unlocks knowledge for next; spatial gates tell story of forbidden knowledge
Human castle, beastmen caves, lizardmen swamp dwellings, Azcan temple, volcano sanctum; architecture communicates civilization level and nature of each culture
Valley with impossibly tall cliffs and red sun establishes this is not natural world; visual impossibility communicates supernatural containment

environmental_storytelling.technique: Item scarcity and purposeful artifact trail
environmental_storytelling.example: Specific artifacts needed to progress through regions; collection mechanic reinforces player as explorer-detective uncovering world design

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active military command; issuing orders to defend castle
Shifts to exploration delegation; requests party find allies; passive leadership
Aggressive siege warfare; final push into castle
Vanishes entirely; no interaction; implies dimensional separation
Neutral to supportive; allow party exploration and departure
Progressive hostility; visual aggression toward party; irrational responses
Complete disappearance; mind control by Burrower; absence of agency
Sole resistant NPC; provides knowledge; remains sane; acts as truth-keeper and guide
Immediately hostile; territorial combat; tribe protection
Hostile but provide knowledge after defeat; transactional relationship established
Temple guardians; hostile to intruders; violence around sacred sites; artifact protection

notes: This game uses a hybrid communication strategy: direct visual signals (red sun, impossible cliffs, combat mode UI) combined with behavioral narrative (NPC corruption arc) to telegraph world-state. The core manifestation layer operates on two axes: (1) Environmental impossibility signaling supernatural conflict and (2) NPC behavior deviation signaling invisible antagonistic forces. Faction identity is communicated entirely through environmental context (biome/architecture) rather than explicit visual markers like color or heraldry, reflecting the game's D&D tabletop heritage where dungeon master narration substitutes for graphical clarity. The corruption mechanic (townspeople going mad) is the game's primary storytelling tool for communicating threat that transcends visible enemies. Tier T3 qualitative assessment sufficient; specific sprite palettes and animation frame counts not documented in available source.
